_provenance.note: A FIFTH literature (Epic AC): online learning / regret minimization (online gradient descent; strongly-convex online learning). Lifted into grounded dgroups with the SAME higher-order CAUSE(premise -> guarantee) schema as the conformal, optimization, learning, and concentration corpora. Used to push the unsupervised role-discovery and cross-domain analogy to FIVE fields with no new design: if the 'no-regret play' relation is discovered to play the same structural-property role (PC/2) as weighted-exchangeability / contraction / uniform-convergence / bounded-martingale, the analogy web extends automatically. Grounded; every symbol is a verbatim substring of its statement, checked by grounding.verify.
_provenance.sources: Zinkevich (2003), 'Online Convex Programming and Generalized Infinitesimal Gradient Ascent' (sublinear regret for OGD under bounded gradients); Hazan, Agarwal & Kale (2007), 'Logarithmic Regret Algorithms for Online Convex Optimization' (curvature/strong convexity sharpens the regret).

online_gradient_descent.name: online_gradient_descent
online_gradient_descent.text: An online loss sequence with bounded gradients drives a no-regret play over the horizon length, and that no-regret play causes a sublinear regret bound on the competitive gap.

online_strong_convexity.name: online_strong_convexity
online_strong_convexity.text: For a strongly convex online loss sequence the curvature bound controls the horizon length; the online loss sequence still has bounded gradients and drives a no-regret play over the horizon length, the curvature bound also causes that no-regret play, and the no-regret play causes a sublinear regret bound on the competitive gap.
